summary: Full backend + frontend test run for Oculux AI Glasses. 20/20 backend pytest tests passed. All frontend flows verified end-to-end (home, shop tier filter, PDP, cart drawer increment/decrement, checkout redirect to live Stripe Sandbox page, register -> /account, AR Try-On UI). Only a minor cosmetic note on Account page name rendering.

issues: body_text search did not match the literal user name 'Oculux UI Tester' (email shows correctly). Likely rendered with different casing or split node — verify visually that the full registered name is displayed.

action_items: (Optional) Confirm the Account page renders the registered user's full name verbatim (e.g. heading text).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~490 lines — acceptable, but if more endpoints are added consider splitting routes into modules (auth, products, checkout, orders).
STRIPE_API_KEY is read at import; good. webhook_url is derived from request.base_url — works in this environment but be aware behind some proxies you may need to honor X-Forwarded-* headers.
current_user silently returns None on any JWT decode error. That's fine for endpoints that allow guests, but consider logging at DEBUG level for diagnosing token issues.
Mongo _id is correctly excluded ({"_id": 0}) on every read — good.
test_credentials: Backend test user (created during run): tester+<random>@oculux.com / oculux-test-2026!. /app/memory/test_credentials.md was empty; no pre-seeded admin required for this app.
seed_data_creation: Backend startup seeds 6 products idempotently. Tests register a fresh tester+<rand>@oculux.com user per run; newsletter signup also uses a unique email per run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests live in /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (pytest, 20 tests covering root, products, auth, newsletter, checkout, orders). Public base URL is REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL from /app/frontend/.env. Critical route note: AR page is /ar-try-on (not /ar). Tier filter buttons are 'All', 'Professional', 'Kids', 'Seniors' — they set ?tier=pro|kids|senior in URL. Add-to-cart on PDP auto-opens the cart drawer. Cart checkout actually redirects the browser to Stripe Checkout — to verify only the API without navigation, listen for the POST /api/checkout/session response BEFORE the navigation completes, or intercept the route.
